description: Curated list of MCP servers Cowork knows how to install and configure. Each entry includes a `spec` that is written directly into the user's ~/.claude.json under `mcpServers`, plus optional `params` (runtime-only values) and `env` (secret/config env vars) the UI must collect before activating.

servers.name: MemeStack
servers.description: Search and browse a curated gallery of memes, infographics, and visual content. Remote server — no install and no credentials required.
servers.notes: Remote HTTPS, no auth, no params. Handy sanity check for the Cowork MCP pipeline.

servers.name: Graphlit
servers.description: Ingest documents, web pages, Slack/Drive/Jira/Linear/GitHub and more into a Graphlit project and query it as a RAG-ready knowledge base.

env.label: Graphlit Organization ID
env.description: Copy from the API settings dashboard on the Graphlit portal.

env.label: Graphlit Environment ID
env.description: Also on the API settings dashboard — sits next to the Organization ID.

env.label: Graphlit JWT secret
env.description: Signs JWTs for the Graphlit API. Copy from the API settings dashboard on the Graphlit portal.

servers.notes: Optional connector credentials (SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, LINEAR_API_KEY, GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN, NOTION_API_KEY, JIRA_EMAIL + JIRA_TOKEN, Google Mail tokens, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, TWITTER_TOKEN) can be added later by editing the entry in ~/.claude.json directly if you want to enable specific data connectors.
